title: rosa tomentosa var. subadenophylla
intro: Rosa tomentosa var. subadenophylla, also known as a variant of the woolly rose, is a species of flowering plant in the family Rosaceae. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, with medicinal properties attributed to its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory compounds. The health benefits of Rosa tomentosa var. subadenophylla include potential uses in wound healing, antimicrobial properties, and anti-aging treatments. Herbal preparations of this plant include teas, infusions, and topical creams, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.
